blocks.text: DTC P0443: Purge Control SOL.V. Failure

The purge control SOL.V. is installed in the negative pressure piping between the canister and the intake manifold. When the engine coolant temperature is below the specified value, the PGM-FI ECU closes the purge control SOL.V. to prevent negative pressure from being applied to the canister. V. (duty control) to adjust the amount of fuel evaporative gas that is sucked into the engine. If the return signal from the purge control SOL.V. remains unchanged for more than a set period of time in response to the duty output, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

Purge Control SOL.V. Output duty [Purge Control SOL V]

blocks.text: Purge When the return signal from the control SOL.V. remains unchanged for more than 5 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (PCS line) between PGM-FI ECU and purge control SOL.
Ground fault in code (PCS line) between PGM-FI ECU and purge control SOL.
Purge Disconnection of control SOL.V. cord (power supply line)

blocks.text: Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
